w:r.w:t: Target user: Recruiting agencies (US and India markets)

This document lists core must-have features, AI-specific features, and good-to-have features for an AI-first SaaS recruiting platform designed for staffing and recruiting agencies. It is intended as a starter input for a detailed product requirements document (PRD).
These are the non-negotiable capabilities that recruiting agencies expect from any modern platform. Without these, the product will not clear evaluation shortlists.
Recruiting agencies juggle dozens of clients simultaneously. The platform must treat the agency-client relationship as a first-class entity.
Separate workspaces per client with custom branding, job templates, and access controls
Agencies need data isolation between competing clients in the same industry
Client-facing portals with the agency’s or client’s branding, logo, and custom domain
Agencies sell a branded service; generic platform branding undermines positioning
Granular permissions: agency admin, recruiter, client hiring manager (view-only or collaborative), candidate
Clients often want pipeline visibility without full platform access
Track usage, interviews, and placements per client for invoicing and margin analysis
Agencies bill per placement, retainer, or hourly; the platform must support all three models
Unlike corporate HR tools, agency platforms must manage candidates as long-term assets placed across multiple clients over months or years.
Single searchable database across all clients with tagging, notes, and interaction history
Agencies re-place candidates; losing context between engagements is costly
Drag-and-drop Kanban boards with configurable stages per client or role type
Each client has different processes: some need 2 rounds, others need 5
CV parsing, skills taxonomy, interview history, assessment scores, and placement history
Recruiters need a 360-degree view before submitting candidates to clients
Automatic flagging of duplicate candidates across the database
Agencies receive thousands of applications; duplicates waste time and embarrass the agency
Centralised email, SMS, and WhatsApp messaging with templates and tracking
Recruiters spend 40%+ of time on candidate communication; it must be unified
Track which candidates have been submitted to which clients, with status and feedback
Prevents double-submissions and provides clear audit trails
Structured intake forms capturing role requirements, skills, compensation, and client preferences
Standardises job intake; reduces back-and-forth with clients
One-click posting to Indeed, LinkedIn, Naukri, Monster, Glassdoor with response tracking
Manual multi-board posting is the biggest time sink for junior recruiters
Hosted, branded career pages per client or for the agency itself
Smaller clients lack their own career pages; agencies can offer this as value-add
Automatic CV/resume parsing into structured profiles with skills extraction
Manual data entry from CVs is error-prone and wastes recruiter time
Calendar sync (Google, Outlook, Exchange), timezone detection, self-service booking links
67% of recruiters spend 30 mins to 2 hours scheduling each interview
Multi-interviewer availability matching, room booking, and agenda distribution
Agency-arranged panel interviews with client teams are logistically complex
Automated reminders via email, SMS, and WhatsApp with one-click reschedule
No-shows waste interviewer time; reminders reduce them by 30-50%
Structured scorecard submission with deadline enforcement and nudges
Agencies need timely client feedback to keep pipelines moving
One-way video with configurable questions, time limits, retake options, and no-login access
Replaces phone screens; candidates complete across timezones on their own time
Built-in video with recording, transcription, and structured scorecard overlay
Agencies conduct interviews on behalf of clients; third-party tools fragment workflow
Pre-built tests: technical skills, cognitive ability, language proficiency, personality
Agencies must validate candidate claims before presenting to clients
Browser-based IDE with 30+ languages, test case execution, and anti-cheating measures
Technical recruiting agencies need coding tests as table stakes
Customisable rating rubrics aligned to job requirements with weighted scoring
Eliminates gut-feel evaluations; improves consistency across recruiters
Tab-switch detection, copy-paste monitoring, AI-response detection, optional webcam proctoring
Cheating rates have doubled since 2024; agencies need credible results
Real-time metrics: time-to-fill, time-in-stage, source effectiveness, pass-through rates
Agencies sell speed; they need to prove it with data to retain clients
Exportable, branded reports: pipeline status, candidate summaries, activity logs
Weekly client updates are standard; manual report assembly is painful
Activity tracking per recruiter: sourcing, submissions, interviews, placements
Agency managers need visibility for coaching and commission calculations
Immutable logs, consent management (GDPR, DPDPA), data retention and deletion workflows
Regulatory exposure is growing; agencies face multiple regimes across US and India
Adverse impact analysis, demographic pass-through rates, bias audit exports
Enterprise clients increasingly require agencies to demonstrate fair hiring
Workday, Greenhouse, Lever, iCIMS, SAP SF, BambooHR, Zoho Recruit, Keka, Darwinbox
Agencies push candidates into client ATS; bidirectional sync is critical
RESTful API with webhooks for custom integrations, data export, and automation
Larger agencies build custom tooling around their core platform
Gmail, Outlook, Twilio SMS, WhatsApp Business API, and calendar sync
All candidate touchpoints must flow through the platform for tracking
SAML/OAuth SSO, 2FA, SOC 2 compliance, data encryption at rest and in transit
Enterprise clients require security certifications before procurement
These AI capabilities define the platform’s competitive differentiation. They are split into essential AI features (required for launch) and high-value AI features (Phase 2 roadmap).
Without these, the platform cannot credibly claim an AI-first positioning against HireVue, Paradox, and Mercor.
LLM-powered agent conducts adaptive two-way interviews (video, voice, or chat) with real-time follow-up questions
Replaces first-round screens entirely; operates 24/7 across timezones
Evaluates responses, assessments, and CV data against requirements; produces explainable composite score
Ranked shortlist instead of manual review; scoring rationale is visible to recruiter and client
NLP-based skills, experience, and qualification extraction; matches candidates to open roles with relevance scores
Surfaces existing database candidates before external sourcing; cuts time-to-shortlist
Ingests JD and produces structured question sets: technical, behavioural, situational
Eliminates hours of prep; ensures consistency across interviewers and roles
Post-interview summaries from transcripts: strengths, concerns, skill gaps, next steps
Structured debriefs instead of raw transcripts or vague notes
AI chatbot (text, WhatsApp, SMS) screens: availability, salary, visa, notice period, qualifications
Handles 100s of inbound applications per role without recruiter time
Monitors scoring for adverse impact across protected categories; generates audit-ready reports
Built-in compliance with NYC LL144 and EU AI Act; demonstrable to enterprise clients
These deepen the AI value proposition and create switching costs. Target the 6-12 month roadmap.
Drafts personalised sourcing messages (email, LinkedIn) based on profile and JD, with A/B testing
5-12% higher response rates with AI personalisation
Models time-to-fill, drop-off probability, offer acceptance likelihood from historical data
Proactively address pipeline risks; give clients realistic timelines
Analyses interviewer patterns: question quality, speaking ratio, bias indicators; suggests improvements
Improves junior recruiter quality; accelerates new hire onboarding
Maps competencies against requirements; identifies gaps with upskilling paths
Positions agency as strategic partner, not just a supplier
Flags disengagement risk, counter-offer likelihood, optimal follow-up timing from communication patterns
Reduces candidate drop-offs during notice periods
Analyses JD for bias, inclusivity, clarity, SEO; suggests improvements with predicted impact
Better JDs attract more diverse, qualified applicants
Detects AI-generated responses, proxy candidates, deepfake video, voice spoofing
Protects agency reputation; fastest-growing buyer requirement
AI agent autonomously sources, screens, schedules, follows up; escalates to recruiter only for exceptions
Moves from tool-based efficiency to outcome-based automation
These enhance appeal and serve as upsell levers, demo differentiators, or retention drivers. Not launch blockers, but should be prioritised based on target segment feedback.
Branded portal for status tracking, document uploads, assessment completion, scheduling, and messaging.
Native or PWA for interview recording, assessments, and messaging. Critical for India where 70%+ of seekers are mobile-first.
AI mock interviews with instant feedback on content, pacing, and clarity. Positions the agency as candidate-friendly.
Platform UI and AI in 10+ languages: Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Spanish for India and US markets.
Post-process surveys with automated analysis. Demonstrate experience quality to clients.
Track commissions, placement fees (percentage, flat, retainer), and revenue per client with automated calculations.
Generate offer letters, manage e-signatures, track acceptance deadlines within the platform.
CRM-style view: active requisitions, pipeline health, SLA adherence, revenue, relationship health score.
Track consultant availability, skills, and auto-match to incoming requirements for bench-model agencies.
Connect with Fieldglass, Beeline for agencies serving managed-services clients.
Benchmark compensation by role, location, and experience. Helps agencies advise clients on competitive offers.
Track cost-per-hire and quality-of-hire by source with attribution modelling.
AI risk scores based on fill rates, response times, engagement. Early warning for at-risk relationships.
Shared notes, @mentions, task assignments, and activity feeds per candidate or requisition.
Shared space for shortlist reviews, candidate feedback, and submission approvals without email.
No-code builder: trigger emails on stage changes, auto-assign candidates, escalate stale pipelines.
Real-time notifications and actions (approve, schedule) within team messaging tools.
These address requirements unique to Indian recruiting and represent significant differentiation opportunities.
Manage TPO relationships, campus drives, bulk testing, and offer rollouts for fresher hiring at scale.
Conduct AI interviews in Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Marathi, and Bengali for non-English pools.
Audio-only mode, compressed video, progressive loading for Tier 2/3 cities.
Deep integration with Zoho Recruit, Keka, Darwinbox, greytHR.
Track 30/60/90-day notice periods, buyout negotiations, and background verification status.
Optional Aadhaar e-KYC with DPDPA-compliant consent management.
The matrix below maps all features against category and recommended release phase. Use as a starting point for sprint planning and roadmap discussions.
Multi-client workspaces, candidate database, pipeline management, CV parsing, job posting, async video, scheduling, scorecards, basic reporting, ATS connectors, SSO
AI conversational interviewer, AI scoring, resume matching, AI questions, screening chatbot, interview summaries, bias monitoring
Live video, coding assessments, full proctoring, advanced reporting, DEI analytics, open API, WhatsApp integration
Predictive analytics, AI outreach, fraud detection, JD optimisation, skills gap analysis
Candidate portal, mobile app, commission tracking, workflow builder, client collaboration, market intelligence
Regional language AI, campus module, low-bandwidth mode, India HRIS, notice period tracking, Aadhaar e-KYC
Agentic workflows, AI coaching, sentiment analysis, client health scoring, competitive intelligence
This starter document is intentionally feature-focused. The detailed PRD should expand each feature with:
Who uses this, what they accomplish, and acceptance criteria.
How each feature affects core entities (candidate, job, client, assessment, interview) and relationships.
GDPR consent flows, DPDPA data minimisation, NYC LL144 audit exports, EU AI Act documentation.
Which features map to which tier (free, standard, premium, enterprise) and usage-based billing metering.
Third-party LLMs vs fine-tuned models vs proprietary training data per capability.
Target latencies, concurrency (e.g. 500 simultaneous AI interviews), and scale requirements.
How implementation differs from incumbents; what the switching-cost story looks like.
Use the companion market research document (AI Interviewing Platforms: A Complete Market Map, April 2026) for competitive benchmarking, regulatory context, and pricing model decisions.
